name: HonorBox
kicker: For everyone whose buyers live on GitHub
headline: Sell digital products with just Stripe + GitHub.
tagline: 0% platform fee, $0/month, no server. A static storefront on GitHub Pages, checkout through your own Stripe account, and a GitHub Action that delivers every sale while you sleep.
subline: This store runs on the engine it sells. Fork the free core and open your own stand.

items.title: Fork the stand
items.text: Fork the free, MIT-licensed engine and edit one config file. Your storefront builds to GitHub Pages — hosting costs $0, forever.

items.title: Put out your goods
items.text: Create a Stripe Payment Link for each product and drop the URL in the config. Buyers pay you directly on your own Stripe account — nobody stands between you and your balance.

items.title: Let the box collect
items.text: A scheduled GitHub Action polls Stripe, invites each buyer's GitHub account to your private product repo, and keeps your books. No webhooks, no server, no database.

sections.text: Who sells this way: indie hackers and SaaS builders, template and boilerplate makers, coding-course creators, CLI and API authors, open-source maintainers funding their work, agencies productizing internal tools, students selling a first project. The one hard requirement: your buyers need GitHub accounts, because delivery is a repo invite. Selling physical goods, ebooks for lay readers, or to a general consumer audience? A merchant-of-record platform will serve you better — the first guide below compares them honestly.

sections.title: What the middlemen charge
sections.note: Fees as published July 2026. HonorBox is not a merchant of record — you sell on your own Stripe account and handle your own tax obligations (see FAQ).
rows: yes — funds go straight to you

items.q: What exactly do I get when I buy?
items.a: Your GitHub account is invited to the product's private repository — usually within minutes, always within a few hours. You keep access permanently, updates included. If the invite doesn't arrive, email honorbox@proton.me or open an issue and it will be fixed or refunded.

items.q: Why is there no instant download?
items.a: Delivery is a private-repo invite because it's the only channel that stays access-controlled — revocable on refund, with updates included. A scheduled job runs the fulfillment; that's the honest cost of a $0/month stack, and it's usually minutes.

items.q: Do my buyers need GitHub accounts?
items.a: Yes. Delivery is a repo invite, so every buyer needs one. Fine for code, templates, courses aimed at technical people, and tools — the wrong channel for lay-reader ebooks, physical goods, or general consumers. Better to know that before your first failed delivery, not after.

items.q: Do I need HonorBox Pro to use the engine?
items.a: No. The free core is a complete store: one theme, checkout, fulfillment, docs. Pro adds the premium themes, multi-product catalog patterns, and the offline license-key module — and buying it funds the free core.

items.q: What about VAT and sales tax?
items.a: HonorBox is not a merchant of record. You sell on your own Stripe account, so tax obligations are yours — fine for most small sellers under registration thresholds, and Stripe Tax can automate it beyond that. The docs cover this honestly instead of pretending it away.
30 days, no questions asked, refunded through Stripe to your original payment method. Repository access ends when a refund is issued.
HonorBox is an independent project. Support: honorbox@proton.me or GitHub issues — both are answered fast.

sections.text: This store dogfoods its own engine — the checkout you go through is the same one your buyers would. If something feels off, that's a bug report we want.
